[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 艳异编 - Title meaning 'Collection of Strange and Beautiful Things,' referring to a compilation of unusual and remarkable stories.
  • 编集 - Means 'compiled and edited,' indicating the editorial work involved in creating the collection.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 艳异编 - Suggests a collection of extraordinary or supernatural stories, popular in Chinese literature during the Ming dynasty.
  • 王世贞 - Prominent literary figure of the Ming dynasty, known for his contributions to classical Chinese literature and his role as a critic and compiler.
  • 正集 续集 - Division of the collection into a main volume and a supplementary volume, a common practice in classical Chinese literature to organize extensive works.
  • 目录 - Table of contents, providing an overview of the structure and content of the collection.

Proper Nouns

  • 艳异编 - Title of the collection, a proper noun referring to a specific work.
  • 正集 续集 - Proper nouns referring to the main and supplementary volumes of the collection.
  • 王世贞 - Name of the compiler, a proper noun referring to a specific historical figure.
  • 编集 - Term for 'compiled and edited,' a proper noun in this context referring to the editorial process.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 艳异编 - A literary anthology that collects rare and exotic stories, often with a focus on the unusual or supernatural.
  • 仙部 - Refers to the section or category of immortals or deities within the anthology.
  • 宫掖部 - Refers to the section or category of imperial harem or palace-related stories within the anthology.
  • 宵游宫 - A palace or place associated with nocturnal activities or events.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 少昊 - A legendary emperor, often associated with the eastern direction and the metal element.
  • 妲已 - A legendary figure, often depicted as a seductress who led to the downfall of the Shang dynasty.
  • 周昭王 - A ruler of the Zhou dynasty, known for his military campaigns.
  • 穆王 - Another ruler of the Zhou dynasty, famous for his travels and encounters with immortals.
  • 褒姒 - A legendary queen of the Zhou dynasty, often associated with the fall of the dynasty.
  • 夏姬 - A legendary figure, often depicted as a seductress with a tragic fate.
  • 越王 - Refers to the rulers of the ancient state of Yue.
  • 燕昭王 - A ruler of the state of Yan, known for his efforts to strengthen his state.
  • 齐襄王 - A ruler of the state of Qi, known for his military campaigns.
  • 春申君 - A prominent statesman and chancellor of the state of Chu.
  • 中山阴后 - A queen consort of the state of Zhongshan.
  • 秦宣太后 - A powerful queen consort of the state of Qin.
  • 吕不韦 - A prominent statesman and chancellor of the state of Qin.
  • 汉武帝 - A ruler of the Han dynasty, known for his military campaigns and cultural achievements.
  • 孝武李夫人传 - A biography of Lady Li, a concubine of the Han emperor Wudi.
  • 王昭君 - A legendary figure, known for her marriage to the Xiongnu chieftain and her role in maintaining peace.
  • 孝成赵皇后传 - A biography of Empress Zhao of the Han dynasty.
  • 赵飞燕外传 - A biography of Empress Zhao Feiyan, known for her beauty and tragic fate.
  • 赵飞燕合德别传 - A separate biography of Empress Zhao Feiyan and her sister Zhao Hede.
  • 飞燕事六条 - Six accounts or stories about Empress Zhao Feiyan.

Proper Nouns

  • 柳毅传 - A biography or story about Liu Yi, a legendary figure known for his encounter with a dragon princess.
  • 灵应传 - A biography or story about a figure associated with spiritual or miraculous events.
  • 裴航 - A legendary figure, often associated with love and supernatural events.
  • 少室仙姝传 - A biography or story about a beautiful and talented woman from the Shaoshi region.
  • 嵩岳嫁女记 - A story about the marriage of a daughter from Mount Song.
  • 裴谌 - A historical or legendary figure, possibly a relative or associate of Pei Hang.
  • 张老 - A historical or legendary figure, possibly a sage or advisor.
  • 薛昭传 - A biography or story about Xue Zhao, a legendary figure.

  • 假令不善读诗者，而徒侈淫哇之词 - Conditional phrase meaning 'if someone is not good at reading poetry and merely indulges in licentious and vulgar words.'
  • 领忘惩创之旨 - '领' means 'to understand' or 'to grasp'; '忘' means 'to forget'; '惩创之旨' refers to the purpose or intent of punishment and correction.
  • 虽多亦奚以为 - '虽多' means 'even if there are many'; '亦奚以为' is a rhetorical question meaning 'what good is it?'
  • 奇而法，正而葩 - '奇而法' means 'strange yet lawful'; '正而葩' means 'righteous yet splendid.'
  • 纖合度，修短中程 - '纖合度' means 'fine and fitting'; '修短中程' means 'long and short within the right measure.'
  • 才情妙敏，踪迹幽玄 - '才情妙敏' means 'talent and emotion are subtle and swift'; '踪迹幽玄' means 'traces are profound and mysterious.'
  • 小言中之白眉者 - '小言' refers to minor or secondary sayings; '白眉' is a metaphor for the best or most outstanding.
  • 我能转法华，不为法华转 - Buddhist phrase meaning 'I can transform the Lotus Sutra, not be transformed by it.'
  • 乐而不淫，哀而不伤 - '乐而不淫' means 'joyful but not excessive'; '哀而不伤' means 'sad but not hurtful.'
  • 纵横流漫而不纳于邪，诡谲浮夸而不离于正 - '纵横流漫' means 'flowing freely'; '不纳于邪' means 'not leading to evil'; '诡谲浮夸' means 'strange and exaggerated'; '不离于正' means 'not deviating from the right path.'
  • 尽信书则不如无书 - '尽信书' means 'to fully trust books'; '则不如无书' means 'is better than having no books.'
  • 不佞，懒如嵇，狂如阮，慢如长卿，迂加元稹 - '不佞' is a self-deprecating term meaning 'not worthy'; '懒如嵇' refers to being lazy like Ji Kang; '狂如阮' refers to being wild like Ruan Ji; '慢如长卿' refers to being slow like Zhang Heng; '迂加元稹' refers to being overly formal like Yuan Zhen.
  • 啸咏时每手一编，未尝不临文感慨，不能喻之于人 - '啸咏' means 'sighing and singing'; '每手一编' means 'always holding a book'; '未尝不临文感慨' means 'never fails to feel moved when reading'; '不能喻之于人' means 'cannot explain it to others.'
  • 窃谓开卷有益，夫固善取益者自为益耳 - '窃谓' means 'I privately think'; '开卷有益' means 'reading is beneficial'; '夫固善取益者自为益耳' means 'those who are good at benefiting themselves will naturally benefit.'

  • 再三邀予作，予不得辞，遂应命作诗曰 - '再三' means 'repeatedly', '邀' means 'invite', '予' means 'I', '不得辞' means 'cannot refuse', '遂' means 'then', '应命' means 'obey the command', and '作诗曰' means 'compose a poem saying'.
  • 香风引到大罗天 - '香风' means 'fragrant wind', '引到' means 'lead to', and '大罗天' means 'Great Luo Heaven'.

  • 别有善笛女子，短发丽衣，貌甚美而目多媚 - '别有' means 'there is another', '善笛' means 'skilled in playing the flute', '短发' means 'short hair', '丽衣' means 'beautiful clothes', '貌甚美' means 'very beautiful appearance', and '目多媚' means 'eyes very charming'.
  • 与潘妃偕来 - '与' means 'with', '潘妃' means 'Lady Pan', and '偕来' means 'come together'.

  • 时令吹笛，往往亦及酒 - '时令' means 'sometimes order', '吹笛' means 'play the flute', '往往' means 'often', and '及酒' means 'and drink'.
  • 太后顾而问曰 - '太后' means 'empress dowager', '顾' means 'look', '而' means 'and', '问' means 'ask', and '曰' means 'say'.
  • 识此否？石家绿珠也 - '识此否' means 'do you recognize this?', '石家' means 'Shi family', and '绿珠' is a historical figure.
  • 潘妃养作妹，故潘妃与俱来 - '养作妹' means 'raised as a sister', '故' means 'so', '潘妃' means 'Lady Pan', and '与俱来' means 'and came together'.
  • 太后因曰 - '太后' means 'empress dowager', '因' means 'then', and '曰' means 'say'.

  • 绿珠乃谢而作诗曰 - '绿珠' is a historical figure, '乃' means 'then', '谢' means 'thank', and '作诗曰' means 'compose a poem saying'.
  • 此日人非昔日人 - '此日' means 'this day', '人非' means 'people are not', and '昔日人' means 'people of the past'.
  • 笛声空怨赵王伦 - '笛声' means 'flute sound', '空怨' means 'empty complaint', and '赵王伦' is a historical figure.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 艳异编卷二水神部 - Title of the second volume of the *Yan Yi Bian*, a collection of strange tales and legends, specifically the section on water deities.
  • 长庆中 - Refers to the Longqing era (821-824) during the reign of Emperor Muzong of Tang.
  • 进士 - Refers to a scholar who has passed the imperial examinations and is eligible for official positions.
  • 番禺 - Ancient city, now part of modern-day Guangzhou, Guangdong Province.

  • 仆从皆逃 - Indicates that all servants and companions have fled, leaving the protagonist alone.

  • 玉龙膏 - A medicinal ointment, possibly with magical properties, as suggested by the context.
  • 能治业疾 - Literally means 'can treat chronic illnesses,' but may also imply a broader range of ailments.
  • 异人 - Refers to someone extraordinary or supernatural.
  • 广利王 - Likely refers to a water deity or a powerful figure in the realm of water spirits.
  • 寒时但出此盒 - Means 'when it is cold, just take out this box,' implying a warming effect.

  • 炉炭 - Refers to a stove or fire, used for heating.
  • 依其言 - Means 'following her advice,' indicating that the protagonist followed the fortune-teller's instructions.
  • 果有黄衣若宦者 - Describes a person dressed in yellow clothing, resembling an official or eunuch.
  • 叩门甚急 - Means 'knocking on the door very urgently,' indicating a sense of urgency.
  • 召张无颇至 - Means 'summoned Zhang Wupu to come,' indicating that the protagonist was called to meet the water deity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 长庆中 - The Longqing era (821-824) was a period of relative peace and prosperity during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 进士 - The imperial examination system was a crucial part of the Tang bureaucracy, and passing the exams was a significant achievement.
  • 番禺 - An important city in southern China, known for its strategic location and economic importance.
  • 府帅改移 - Administrative changes were common during the Tang Dynasty, reflecting the dynamic nature of the government.
  • 逆旅 - Inns and lodging places were essential for travelers and officials on the move.
  • 仆从皆逃 - This reflects the precarious nature of life during times of political or social upheaval.
  • 善易者 - Fortune-telling and divination were popular practices in ancient China, often used to predict the future or provide guidance.
  • 玉龙膏 - Medicinal ointments with magical or healing properties were a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 能治业疾 - Chronic illnesses were a significant concern in ancient Chinese society, and treatments were often sought through traditional medicine.
  • 异人 - Supernatural beings or extraordinary individuals were a common element in Chinese mythology and folklore.
  • 广利王 - Water deities were often venerated in Chinese culture, particularly in regions near rivers and seas.
  • 寒时但出此盒 - The idea of a warming ointment suggests a practical application of the magical properties described.
  • 炉炭 - Stoves and fires were essential for heating and cooking in ancient China.
  • 依其言 - Following the advice of a trusted source was a common practice in ancient Chinese society.
  • 果有黄衣若宦者 - Yellow clothing was often associated with officials or eunuchs, indicating a high social status.
  • 叩门甚急 - Urgent knocking on the door suggests a sense of importance or urgency.
  • 召张无颇至 - Being summoned by a powerful figure, such as a water deity, indicates a significant event or transformation.

Proper Nouns

  • 艳异编卷二水神部 - Title of the second volume of the *Yan Yi Bian*, a collection of strange tales and legends, specifically the section on water deities.
  • 长庆中 - Era name of the Tang Dynasty; rendered as Longqing.
  • 进士 - Scholar who has passed the imperial examinations; rendered as Jinshi.

  • 善易者 - Skilled fortune-teller or diviner; rendered as Shan Yi Zhe.
  • 袁大娘 - Name of the fortune-teller; rendered as Yuan Dainiang.
  • 玉龙膏 - Medicinal ointment; rendered as Yu Long Gao.
  • 能治业疾 - Can treat chronic illnesses; rendered as Neng Zhi Ye Ji.
  • 异人 - Extraordinary or supernatural individual; rendered as Yi Ren.
  • 广利王 - Water deity or powerful figure; rendered as Guangli Wang.
  • 寒时但出此盒 - When it is cold, just take out this box; rendered as Han Shi Dan Chu Ci He.
  • 寒时 - Cold weather or conditions; rendered as Han Shi.
  • 暄热 - Warm or comfortable; rendered as Xuan Re.
  • 炉炭 - Stove or fire; rendered as Lu Tan.
  • 依其言 - Following her advice; rendered as Yi Qi Yan.
  • 果有黄衣若宦者 - Person dressed in yellow clothing, resembling an official or eunuch; rendered as Guo You Huang Yi Ruo Huan Zhe.
  • 叩门甚急 - Knocking on the door very urgently; rendered as Kou Men Shen Ji.
  • 召张无颇至 - Summoned Zhang Wupu to come; rendered as Zhao Zhang Wu Po Zhi.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 無颇睹贵主华艳动人，颇思之 - 无颇 is a name, 贵主 refers to a noble lady or princess, 华艳动人 means beautiful and captivating, and 颇思之 means thought of her greatly.
  • 月余，忽有青衣叩门而送红笺 - 月余 means more than a month, 青衣 refers to a servant or messenger dressed in blue, 叩门 means knocking on the door, and 红笺 means red paper (likely a letter or note).
  • 无颇捧之，青衣倏亦不见 - 捧之 means holding it, and 倏亦不见 means disappeared suddenly.

  • 情愁无意整花钿 - 情愁 means emotional sorrow, 无意 means without intention, and 整花钿 means arranging the flower ornaments.

  • 无颇拜之 - 拜之 means bowed to her.

  • 心有击触而复作焉 - 心有击触 means heart was struck or hurt, and 复作焉 means it happened again.
  • 若再饵药，当去根干耳 - 若再饵药 means if another dose of medicine is taken, and 当去根干耳 means it will be completely cured.

  • 精贯神祗，至诚感应，遂感水神，持诣水府 - Phrase meaning 'with his sincere devotion, he moved the gods, and the water god took him to the water palace,' where '精贯神祗' means 'his spirit moved the gods,' '至诚感应' means 'sincere devotion elicited a response,' and '水神' means 'water god.'
  • 府君览之，召溺者数辈曰 - Phrase meaning 'the official looked at it and called several drowning people, saying,' where '府君' means 'official' or 'municipal lord,' and '溺者' means 'drowning people.'
  • 谁是郑生所爱 - Question asking who among the drowning people was loved by Zheng, '郑生' refers to Zheng, the protagonist.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 劍乃金 - Straightforward statement that the sword is made of metal.
  • 金既克木而不相生 - Reference to the Five Elements theory where metal (金) overcomes wood (木) but does not generate it.
  • 焉能變化 - Rhetorical question implying that the sword cannot change or transform.
  • 豈同雀入水為蛤，雉入水為蜃哉 - Comparison using a well-known mythological transformation, where birds turn into shells when entering water.
  • 寶劍靈物 - Describes the sword as a spiritual or magical object.
  • 金水相生而入水 - Refers to the interaction between metal and water, suggesting a harmonious relationship.
  • 雷生自不能沉於泉耳 - Implies that the sword, being associated with thunder, cannot sink in water.
  • 後搜劍不獲，乃妄言為龍 - States that after searching for the sword without finding it, people falsely claimed it turned into a dragon.
  • 雷焕只言化去，张司空但言终合 - Refers to historical figures who only mentioned the sword disappearing or reuniting, not transforming into a dragon.
  • 劍之靈異，亦人之鼓铸锻炼，非自然之物 - Suggests that the sword's mystical properties are due to human craftsmanship.
  • 是知终不能为龙，明矣 - Concludes that the sword cannot become a dragon.
  • 梭化为龙如何 - Question about whether a shuttle (梭) can transform into a dragon.
  • 梭，木也 - States that the shuttle is made of wood.
  • 龙本属木，变化归本 - Explains that dragons are associated with wood, and transformations return to their original form.
  • 无信造作，皆梁朝四公诞妄之词耳 - Dismisses the idea as fabricated and baseless, attributing it to the four officials of the Liang Dynasty.
  • 龙之嗜燕血 - Refers to the belief that dragons like to eat swan blood.
  • 龙之清虚，食饮沆瀣 - Describes dragons as pure and ethereal, drinking mist and dew.
  • 若食燕血，岂能行藏 - Suggests that eating swan blood would impair a dragon's ability to move and hide.
  • 盖嗜者乃蛟蜃辈耳 - Explains that those who eat swan blood are likely to be lesser creatures like water dragons or shellfish.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁朝四公 - Refers to four officials of the Liang Dynasty, known for their fabricated stories about dragons.
  • 雷焕 - A historical figure who is said to have discovered and later lost the famous sword, Gan Jiang.
  • 张司空 - Another historical figure mentioned in the context of the sword's disappearance and reuniting.

  • 张司空 - Another historical figure mentioned in the context of the sword.
  • 梁朝四公 - Four officials of the Liang Dynasty, known for their fabricated stories.

  • 澄其神而归寂灭 - Means 'it clarifies its spirit and returns to stillness and extinction,' describing a state of spiritual purity and dissolution.

  • 若未胚，若未凝结，如物在恍惚，精奇杳冥 - Means 'it is neither formed nor solidified, like an object in a state of ambiguity, subtle and mysterious,' describing an indistinct and ethereal state.
  • 百骸五体 - Refers to the body's hundred bones and five limbs, used to describe the entirety of the physical body.
  • 还原返本之术 - Means 'the technique of returning to the original and fundamental state,' describing a method of achieving a pure and original state.
  • 与造化争功 - Means 'to compete with the forces of nature,' describing a state of equivalence with natural processes.

  • 何故不使知 - Means 'why not let him know?'
  • 以其勇過人耳 - Means 'because he is exceptionally brave.'
  • 昔堯遭洪水九年者，乃此子一怒也 - Means 'the nine-year flood that Emperor Yao faced was due to this son's anger.'
  • 近與天將失意，穿其五山 - Means 'recently, he lost favor with the heavenly generals and dug through the Five Mountains.'
  • 上帝以寡人有薄德於古今，遂寬其同氣之罪 - Means 'the Supreme Deity, considering my slight virtue in ancient and modern times, has thus forgiven his sins.'
  • 然猶摩系於此 - Means 'however, he is still bound here.'
  • 故錢塘之人，日來候焉 - Means 'therefore, the people of Qiantang come to visit daily.'
  • 詞未畢，而大聲忽發，天坼地裂，宮殿擺簸，雲煙沸湧 - Means 'before the words were finished, a loud sound suddenly erupted, the sky split and the earth cracked, the palace shook, and clouds and smoke surged.'
  • 俄有赤龍長萬餘尺，電目血舌，朱鱗火鬚；項掣金鎖，鎖牽玉柱；千雷萬霆，繳繞其身，霰雪雨雹，一瞬皆下，乃孽青天而飛去 - Means 'soon, a red dragon appeared, over ten thousand feet long, with electric eyes and a bloody tongue, red scales and fiery whiskers; its neck pulled a golden chain, which was tied to a jade pillar; thousands of thunderbolts and flashes surrounded its body, and hail, snow, and rain fell in an instant, then it flew away into the blue sky.'
  • 毅初恐蹶仆地，君親起持之曰 - Means 'Yi was initially terrified and fell to the ground, but the lord personally got up and held him, saying.'
  • 元惧，固無害 - Means 'do not fear, there is no harm.'
  • 毅良久安抑，乃獲自定，因告辭曰 - Means 'after a long while, Yi calmed down and regained his composure, then he took leave, saying.'
  • 願得生歸，以避復來 - Means 'I wish to return alive to avoid coming back.'
  • 不必如此，其去則然，其來則不爾 - Means 'it is not necessary, when he leaves it is so, but when he comes, it is not.'
  • 幸為少盡缱綣 - Means 'please spend a little more time together.'
  • 因命酌，互舉以人事 - Means 'he then ordered wine to be served, and they drank together, discussing human affairs.'

  • 堯 - Refers to Emperor Yao, one of the legendary Three Sovereigns and Five Emperors of ancient China, known for his virtue and governance.
  • 五山 - Refers to the Five Great Mountains of China, which are Mount Tai, Mount Hua, Mount Song, Mount Heng (in Hunan), and Mount Heng (in Shanxi).
  • 上帝 - Refers to the Supreme Deity or Heaven, a concept in Chinese mythology and religion.
  • 赤龍 - Refers to a red dragon, often associated with the Emperor in Chinese mythology, symbolizing power and authority.
  • 千雷萬霆 - Refers to thousands of thunderbolts, a powerful and awe-inspiring natural phenomenon.
  • 霰雪雨雹 - Refers to hail, snow, and rain, a combination of weather phenomena.

Proper Nouns

  • 錢塘 - Qiantang, an ancient name for Hangzhou.
  • 堯 - Emperor Yao, a legendary Chinese emperor.
  • 五山 - The Five Great Mountains of China.
  • 上帝 - The Supreme Deity or Heaven.

  • 鼓以鳞须，被的云雨 - Metaphorical language describing someone who is fierce or powerful, using '鳞须' (scales and whiskers) and '被的云雨' (clothed in clouds and rain) to create an image of a mythical or supernatural being.

  • 尽五常之至性，穷百行之微旨 - Refers to fully embodying the highest virtues (五常: benevolence, righteousness, propriety, wisdom, and faithfulness) and exploring the subtle meanings of all actions (百行: a hundred behaviors or actions).
  • 乘酒假气，将迫于人 - Describes someone who is drunk and using their strength to threaten others, using '乘酒' (drunk) and '假气' (using strength) to convey a sense of recklessness.
  • 惟王筹之耳 - Polite way of saying 'it is up to you to decide,' using '惟' (only) and '筹之' (consider it) to show deference.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 迩者词述狂狷 - 迩者 means recently or lately, 词述狂狷 refers to speaking or writing in a bold or unrestrained manner.
  • 唐突高明 - 唐突 means to be bold or impertinent, 高明 refers to sages or wise people. Together, it means to be bold or impertinent toward sages.
  • 退自循顾 - 退自 means retreating or reflecting on oneself, 循顾 means to examine or reflect. Together, it means to reflect on oneself.
  • 戾不容责 - 戾 means sin or offense, 不容责 means cannot be blamed. Together, it means cannot be blamed for sins.
  • 幸君子不为此乖间也 - 幸 means fortunately, 君子 means gentleman or virtuous person, 不为 means not to do, 此 means this, 乖间 means to cause discord, 也 is a sentence-ending particle. Together, it means fortunately, the gentleman does not cause discord.
  • 其夕复与欢宴 - 其夕 means that evening, 复 means again, 与 means with, 欢宴 means a joyful feast. Together, it means that evening, they again had a joyful feast.
  • 其乐如旧 - 其 means their, 乐 means joy, 如 means like, 旧 means old or former. Together, it means their joy was as before.
  • 洞庭君夫人别宴毅于潜景殿 - 洞庭君夫人 refers to the wife of the Lord of Dongting Lake, 别宴 means a farewell feast, 毅 is the name of the protagonist, 于 means at, 潜景殿 is the name of a palace. Together, it means the wife of the Lord of Dongting Lake held a farewell feast for Yi at the Palace of Hidden Light.
  • 男女仆妾，悉出预会 - 男女仆妾 means male and female servants, 悉 means all, 出 means to come out, 预会 means to participate in the gathering. Together, it means all male and female servants came out to participate in the gathering.
  • 夫人泣谓毅曰 - 夫人 means lady, 泣 means to weep, 谓 means to say, 曰 means said. Together, it means the lady wept and said to Yi.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 告彭蠡、洞庭 - Means 'to summon Pengli and Dongting,' referring to the lakes. '告' implies a call or summons.
  • 召凌水、羅水 - Means 'to summon Ling Water and Luo Water,' referring to rivers. '召' is another form of summoning.
  • 率維揚之輕鋭，征八水之鷹揚 - Means 'to lead the light and sharp troops of Weiyang and to summon the fierce troops of the eight waters.' '輕鋭' refers to agile and sharp troops, while '鷹揚' describes fierce and powerful troops.
  • 然後檄馮夷，說巨靈 - Means 'then issue orders to Fengyi and persuade Juling.' '檄' is a formal summons, and '說' means to persuade or convince.
  • 鼓子胥之波濤，顯陽侯之鬼怪 - Means 'to stir the waves of Zixu and to reveal the ghosts and monsters of Yang Hou.' '鼓' means to stir or rouse, and '顯' means to reveal or display.
  • 鞭驅列缺，指揮豐隆 - Means 'to whip and drive the thunderbolts and to command Fenglong.' '鞭驅' implies driving with a whip, and '指揮' means to command or direct.
  • 扇疾風，翻暴浪 - Means 'to fan the fierce winds and overturn the violent waves.' '扇' means to fan or stir, and '翻' means to overturn or turn over.
  • 百道俱進，六師鼓行 - Means 'to advance on all fronts and for the six armies to march in unison.' '百道俱進' describes a multi-front advance, and '六師鼓行' refers to the disciplined movement of the army.
  • 一戰而成功 - Means 'to achieve success in a single battle.' '一戰' emphasizes the brevity of the conflict.
  • 則朝那一鱗，立為齑粉 - Means 'then the scales of that fish will be instantly reduced to powder.' '朝那' means 'then,' '鱗' refers to the scales of a fish, and '齑粉' means 'powder.'
  • 泾城千里，坐變污潴 - Means 'the city of Jing will be transformed into a swamp over a thousand miles.' '坐變' means 'instantly transformed,' and '污潴' refers to a swamp or stagnant water.
  • 言下可观，安敢谬矣 - Means 'it can be seen from these words, how could I dare to be mistaken?' '言下' means 'from these words,' and '安敢' means 'how could I dare.'
  • 顷者，泾阳君与洞庭外祖，世为姻戚 - Means 'recently, the Lord of Jingyang and the Lord of Dongting, who are related by marriage, have been bound by kinship.' '顷者' means 'recently,' '世为姻戚' means 'have been bound by kinship through marriage.'
  • 后以琴瑟不调，弃掷少妇 - Means 'later, due to discord in their relationship, they abandoned the young wife.' '琴瑟不调' is a metaphor for discord in a marriage.
  • 遭钱塘之一怒，伤生害稼，怀山襄陵，泾水穷鳞，寻毙外祖之牙齿 - Means 'they faced the anger of Qiantang, which caused harm to life and crops, and the mountains and hills were submerged, and the fish in Jing River perished, leading to the death of the teeth of the grandfather.' '钱塘之一怒' refers to the power of Qiantang, and '怀山襄陵' means 'mountains and hills were submerged.'
  • 今泾上车轮马迹犹在，史传具存，固非谬也 - Means 'now the wheel tracks and horse prints on the Jing River are still there, and the historical records are all present, so it is not a mistake.' '史传具存' means 'the historical records are all present.'
  • 妾又以夫族得罪于天，未蒙上帝昭雪 - Means 'I have also been wronged by heaven through my husband's family, and I have not yet been cleared by the Heavenly Deity.' '昭雪' means 'to clear a wrong.'
  • 所以销声避影，而自困如是 - Means 'that is why I have become silent and hidden, and have found myself in such a predicament.' '销声避影' means 'to become silent and hidden.'
  • 君若不悉诚款，终以多事为词 - Means 'if you do not fully understand my sincerity, you will ultimately use many matters as an excuse.' '悉诚款' means 'fully understand my sincerity,' and '多事为词' means 'use many matters as an excuse.'
  • 则向者之言，不敢避上帝之责也 - Means 'then the words I spoke earlier, I dare not avoid the responsibility of the Heavenly Deity.' '向者之言' means 'the words I spoke earlier,' and '上帝之责' means 'the responsibility of the Heavenly Deity.'
  • 宝遂许诺 - Means 'Bao then agreed.' '遂许诺' means 'then agreed.'
  • 卒爵撤馔，再拜而去 - Means 'he finished his wine, removed the food, bowed twice, and left.' '卒爵' means 'finished his wine,' and '再拜而去' means 'bowed twice and left.'
  • 宝及晡方寤，耳闻目览，恍然如在 - Means 'Bao woke up at dusk, hearing and seeing everything, feeling as if he was still there.' '及晡方寤' means 'woke up at dusk,' and '恍然如在' means 'feeling as if he was still there.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The couplet '一饮琼浆百感生，玄霜捣尽见云英' uses parallelism and metaphor to describe the process of refining something rare and the emotional impact of doing so.
  • The couplet '蓝桥便是神仙窟，何必崎岖上玉京' uses a metaphorical comparison to suggest that one does not need to endure great difficulties to achieve something noble or divine.
  • The phrase '遂饰装归' is elliptical and could be expanded to 'then dressed up and returned to the imperial carriage station.'
  • The phrase '辇下经蓝桥驿侧近' is elliptical and could be expanded to 'near the Blue Bridge Post at the imperial carriage station, he encountered...'
  • The phrase '因渴甚，遂下道求浆而饮' is elliptical and could be expanded to 'because of extreme thirst, he descended the road to seek water to drink and found...'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 弄玉有夫皆得道，劉綱兼室盡登仙 - This parallel structure contrasts two pairs of immortals, emphasizing their shared attainment of immortality.
  • 君能細細窺朝露，須逐雲車拜洞天 - The sentence is elliptical, with '君' (you) implied as the subject, and the two clauses linked by the necessity of following the cloud carriage.
  • 陟覽之，又不過意 - The sentence is elliptical, with '陟覽之' (he looked at it) and '又不過意' (still felt unsatisfied) forming a contrast.
  • 姝又至，柔容冶態，靚衣明眸 - The sentence is a descriptive catalogue of the beauty's features, with each clause adding detail.

  • 相者谁 - A question meaning 'who is the matchmaker?'
  • 侍者谁 - A question meaning 'who is the attendant?'
  • 弹筝击筑者谁 - A question meaning 'who is the zither player and the zhu player?' The zhu is a stringed instrument similar to a lute.
  • 幄中坐者谁 - A question meaning 'who is sitting in the tent?'
  • 西王母 - The Queen Mother of the West, a prominent figure in Chinese mythology.

  • 水精方盘 - A crystal square tray, symbolizing purity and elegance.
  • 瑶幄 - A jade tent, indicating a luxurious and sacred space.
  • 卫符卿、李八百 - Historical or legendary figures, possibly known for their wisdom or magical abilities.

  • 茅盈 - A legendary figure associated with Taoist immortality.
  • 麻姑 - A legendary immortal known for her longevity and magical powers.
  • 谢自然 - A legendary figure associated with Taoist practices and immortality.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 俄有一人，驾鹤而来 - A classical expression meaning 'Soon, a person arrived riding on a crane,' symbolizing a divine or immortal figure.
  • 久望 - Means 'long-awaited' or 'long-expected.'
  • 礼生来未 - A formal inquiry meaning 'Has the official of ritual arrived yet?'
  • 碧玉堂 - A term for a jade-green hall, often used metaphorically to describe a splendid or sacred place.
  • 适缘莲花峰士奏章 - Means 'Just because the official of the Lotus Peak submitted a memorial.'
  • 事须决遣 - Means 'matters that need to be decided and dispatched.'
  • 尚多未来客 - Means 'there are still many guests who have not arrived.'
  • 奏章事者，有何所为 - Means 'What is the purpose of the memorial?'
  • 浮梁县令宋延年 - Refers to the county magistrate of Fuliang named Song Yan'Nian.
  • 贿赂履官途 - Means 'accepting bribes on the path of officialdom.'
  • 苛虐为官政 - Means 'cruel and harsh governance.'
  • 生情于案犊 - Means 'fabricating cases or documents.'
  • 忠恕之道蔑闻 - Means 'the way of loyalty and forgiveness is unheard of.'
  • 惟杂于货财 - Means 'only mixed with wealth and goods.'
  • 巧伪之计更作 - Means 'ingenious deceitful plans are further concocted.'
  • 自贻覆 - Means 'self-inflicted downfall.'
  • 以促余龄 - Means 'to shorten the remaining years of life.'
  • 莲华峰叟受托于人 - Means 'the old man of the Lotus Peak was entrusted by someone.'
  • 奏章甚恳 - Means 'the memorial is very earnest.'
  • 特缓死限，量延五年 - Means 'especially to delay the deadline of death, extending it by five years.'
  • 李君来何迟 - Means 'Why did Mr. Li come so late?'
  • 为敕龙神设水旱之计 - Means 'to instruct the dragon gods to arrange for water and drought.'
  • 作猕淮蔡 - Means 'to create chaos in Huai and Cai.'
  • 以歼妖逆 - Means 'to eliminate evil rebels.'
  • 奈百姓何 - Means 'What about the common people?'
  • 上帝亦有此间 - Means 'the Supreme Deity also has this place.'
  • 予一表断其惑矣 - Means 'I will submit a memorial to dispel their confusion.'
  • 可得闻乎 - Means 'Can I hear it?'
  • 不能悉记，略举大纲耳 - Means 'I cannot remember everything, just the main points.'
  • 表云 - Means 'The memorial says.'
  • 某孙某，克丕业 - Means 'A certain descendant, who has inherited the great cause.'
  • 德洽兆庶 - Means 'virtue reaches the common people.'
  • 临履深薄 - Means 'approaching with humility and respect.'
  • 匪敢怠荒 - Means 'not daring to be lazy or neglectful.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不勞師車 - Literally 'without tiring the army's vehicles,' meaning without mobilizing the military.
  • 平中夏、西蜀之孽 - '平' means to pacify or quell, '中夏' refers to the Central Plains, '西蜀' refers to Western Shu, and '孽' means rebels or rebels' forces.
  • 不费天府 - '不费' means without spending, '天府' refers to the capital or a place of great wealth and resources.
  • 扫东吴、上党之妖 - '扫' means to sweep away or eliminate, '东吴' refers to Eastern Wu, '上党' refers to a region in Shanxi, and '妖' means evil forces or rebels.
  • 九在已见其廓清 - '九在' refers to the nine regions or territories, '廓清' means to clear or pacify.
  • 一方尚屯其气 - '一方' means one region, '屯' means to gather or accumulate, '气' likely refers to evil or rebellious energy.
  • 虺蜴肆毒痛于淮蔡 - '虺蜴' refers to venomous snakes, '肆毒' means to unleash poison, '淮蔡' refers to Huai and Cai regions.
  • 豺狼尚惜其口喙 - '豺狼' refers to wolves, '惜' means to be reluctant, '口喙' means to speak or talk.
  • 蝼蚁犹固其封疆 - '蝼蚁' refers to ants, '固' means to fortify, '封疆' means territory.
  • 若遣时丰人安 - '若遣' means if, '时丰' means good harvest, '人安' means people are at peace.
  • 是稔群丑 - '是' means thus, '稔' means to cultivate or nurture, '群丑' means evil forces or rebels.
  • 但使年饥疠作 - '但使' means if only, '年饥' means famine, '疠作' means epidemics occur.
  • 必摇人心 - '必' means certainly, '摇人心' means to shake people's hearts or morale.
  • 如此倒戈而攻 - '如此' means in this way, '倒戈' means to turn one's weapons against one's own side, '攻' means to attack.
  • 可以席卷 - '可以' means can, '席卷' means to sweep away or conquer.
  • 祸三州之逆党 - '祸' means disaster, '三州' refers to three regions, '逆党' means rebel groups.
  • 所损至微 - '所损' means the damage, '至微' means minimal.
  • 安六合之疾田亡 - '安' means to pacify, '六合' refers to the whole world, '疾田亡' likely refers to the rapid loss of farmland.
  • 其利则厚 - '其利' means the benefit, '则厚' means is substantial.
  • 伏请神龙施水 - '伏请' means humbly request, '神龙' refers to a divine dragon, '施水' means to send rain.
  • 厉鬼行灾 - '厉鬼' refers to malevolent spirits, '行灾' means to cause disasters.
  • 由此天诛 - '由此' means because of this, '天诛' means divine punishment.
  • 以资战力 - '以' means to, '资' means support, '战力' means military strength.
  • 表至嘉，第既允许，可以前贺诛锄矣 - '表至嘉' means the memorial is excellent, '第既允许' means it has been approved, '可以前贺诛锄矣' means we can now celebrate the eradication of the rebels.
  • 此开元、天宝太平之主也 - '此' means this, '开元、天宝' refer to the reigns of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, '太平之主' means a ruler of peace.
  • 未顷，闻萧韶自空而下，执绎节者前唱言 - '未顷' means shortly, '闻' means heard, '萧韶' refers to a wind instrument, '自空而下' means descending from the sky, '执绎节者' means the person holding the ceremonial staff, '前唱言' means announced in advance.
  • 穆天子来 - '穆天子' refers to Emperor Mu of Zhou.
  • 奏乐，群仙皆起 - '奏乐' means to play music, '群仙' means the immortals.
  • 王母避位，拜迎二主 - '王母' refers to the Queen Mother of the West, '避位' means to step aside, '拜迎' means to bow and welcome, '二主' means two rulers.
  • 降阶人幄，环坐而饮 - '降阶' means to descend the steps, '人幄' means to enter the tent, '环坐而饮' means to sit in a circle and drink.
  • 何不拉取老轩辕来 - '何不' means why not, '拉取' means to bring, '老轩辕' refers to the Yellow Emperor.
  • 他今夕主张月宫之宴 - '他' means he, '今夕' means tonight, '主张' means to host, '月宫' means the Moon Palace, '之宴' means the banquet.
  • 非不勤请耳 - '非不' means not because, '勤请' means to invite earnestly, '耳' means (only) that.
  • 瑶池一别后，陵谷几迁移 - '瑶池' refers to the Jade Pool, '一别后' means after parting, '陵谷' means mountains and valleys, '几迁移' means have changed many times.
  • 向来观洛阳东城，已丘墟矣 - '向来' means previously, '观' means to see, '洛阳东城' refers to the eastern part of Luoyang, '已丘墟矣' means has become ruins.
  • 定鼎门西路，忽焉复新 - '定鼎门西路' refers to the western road of Dingding Gate, '忽焉' means suddenly, '复新' means has been rebuilt.
  • 市朝云改，名利如旧，可以悲叹耳 - '市朝' means marketplace and court, '云改' means have changed, '名利' means fame and profit, '如旧' means remain unchanged, '可以悲叹耳' means it is worth lamenting.
  • 穆王把酒，请王母歌 - '穆王' refers to Emperor Mu of Zhou, '把酒' means to hold wine, '请王母歌' means to ask the Queen Mother to sing.
  • 以珊瑚钩击盘而歌曰 - '以' means using, '珊瑚钩' refers to a coral hook, '击盘' means to strike the plate, '而歌曰' means and sang, saying.
  • 劝君酒，为君悲且吟 - '劝君酒' means to encourage you to drink, '为君悲且吟' means to grieve and sing for you.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 中夏 - Refers to the Central Plains, the heartland of China.
  • 西蜀 - Refers to Western Shu, a region in present-day Sichuan.
  • 东吴 - Refers to Eastern Wu, one of the Three Kingdoms.
  • 上党 - Refers to a region in present-day Shanxi.
  • 淮蔡 - Refers to the Huai and Cai regions, in present-day Henan and Anhui.
  • 开元、天宝 - Refers to the reigns of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, known for the prosperity of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 穆天子 - Refers to Emperor Mu of Zhou, a legendary ruler.
  • 王母 - Refers to the Queen Mother of the West, a figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 瑶池 - Refers to the Jade Pool, a mythical place in Chinese mythology.
  • 洛阳 - Refers to Luoyang, an ancient capital of China.
  • 定鼎门 - Refers to Dingding Gate, a gate in the city of Luoyang.
  • 珊瑚钩 - Refers to a coral hook, a decorative item.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 不劳师车，平中夏、西蜀之孽 - This sentence must be read as a statement of pacifying rebels without mobilizing the military.
  • 不费天府，扫东吴、上党之妖 - This sentence must be read as a statement of eliminating evil forces without spending resources.
  • 九在已见其廓清，一方尚屯其气 - This sentence must be read as a statement of clearing or pacifying the nine regions or territories, while one region still gathers evil or rebellious energy.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自从频见市朝改 - Since frequently seeing the changes in the world of politics and commerce; indicates a sense of change and instability in the political and social spheres.
  • 无复瑶池宴乐心 - No longer have the heart for the feasts and pleasures of the Jade Pool; suggests a loss of interest or ability to enjoy the luxurious and divine pleasures.
  • 奉君酒，休叹市朝非 - Presenting your wine, do not lament the changes in the world of politics and commerce; a rhetorical statement encouraging the recipient to accept the current state of affairs.
  • 早知无复瑶池兴，悔驾骅骝草草归 - If I had known there would be no more pleasure in the Jade Pool, I would have regretted riding the swift horse and returning hastily; expresses regret for not fully enjoying the past pleasures.
  • 八马回乘漫风，犹思往事憩昭宫 - Riding back on eight horses with the wind, still thinking of past events in the palace of light; describes a journey back to a place of memory and reflection.
  • 宴移玄圃情方洽，乐奏钧天曲未终 - The feast moves to the Garden of Mystery, and the emotions become more harmonious; the music of the Celestial Court is not yet finished; describes a transition and continuation of a grand feast.
  • 斜汉露凝残月冷，流霞杯泛曙光红 - The oblique Milky Way, dew condenses on the cold crescent moon; the flowing clouds in the cup reflect the red dawn light; describes a poetic scene of dawn.
  • 昆仑回首不知处，疑是酒酣魂梦中 - Looking back at Kunlun, I do not know where it is; it seems to be in the midst of drunkenness and dreams; suggests a sense of disorientation and dream-like state.
  • 一曲笙歌瑶水滨，曾留逸足驻征轮 - A song of flutes and zithers by the banks of the Jade Water, where the swift horse once stopped its journey; describes a past event of stopping at a beautiful place.
  • 人间甲子周千岁，灵境杯筋初一巡 - In the human world, the cycle of the sixty-year period has gone through a thousand years; in the spiritual realm, the cups and chopsticks have just been circulated once; contrasts the passage of time in the human and spiritual worlds.
  • 玉兔银河终不夜，奇花好树镇长春 - The jade rabbit and the Milky Way never see night; the strange flowers and good trees always remain in spring; describes a timeless, idyllic scene.
  • 悄知穆满饶词句，歌向俗流疑误人 - Quietly knowing that Mu Man has many poetic lines, singing to the common people might mislead them; suggests a concern about the impact of poetic expression on the common people.
  • 五十余年四海清，自亲丹灶得长生 - For more than fifty years, the seas are clear; by personally tending the alchemical furnace, one gains immortality; describes a long period of peace and the pursuit of immortality.
  • 若言尽是仙桃力，看取神仙簿上名 - If it is said that all this is due to the power of the peach of immortality, look at the list of immortals; suggests that the achievements are not solely due to the peach of immortality.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 月照骊山露泣花 - Poetic expression where the moonlight on Li Mountain makes the flowers seem to weep with dew.
  • 似悲先帝早升遐 - Idiomatic phrase suggesting that the scene seems to mourn the early death of the former emperor.
  • 至今犹有长生鹿 - Metaphorical expression implying that even now there are deer that seem immortal.
  • 时绕温泉望翠华 - Poetic description of deer frequently circling around hot springs, looking towards the imperial carriage.
  • 帝持杯久之 - Colloquial expression indicating that the emperor held the cup for a long time.
  • 应须召叶静能来唱一曲 - Formal request to summon Ye Jingneng to sing a song.
  • 叙当时事 - Colloquial phrase meaning to narrate events of that time.
  • 幽蓟烟尘别九重 - Poetic expression referring to the departure of the imperial court due to the smoke and dust of war in You and Jizhou.
  • 贵妃汤殿罢歌钟 - Poetic description of the cessation of music and singing in the imperial bathhouse after the departure of the concubine.
  • 中宵扈从无全仗 - Colloquial phrase indicating that during the night, the guards were not fully armed.
  • 大驾苍黄发六龙 - Poetic expression describing the hasty departure of the imperial procession.
  • 妆匣尚留金翡翠 - Poetic description of the jewelry box still containing golden pheasants.
  • 暖池犹浸玉芙蓉 - Poetic description of the warm pool still holding jade lotuses.
  • 荆棒一闭朝元路 - Metaphorical expression suggesting that the path to the imperial court was closed by thorny branches.
  • 惟有悲凤吹晚松 - Poetic expression indicating that only the sad sound of the phoenix blowing through the evening pines remains.
  • 黄龙持杯，立于车前，再拜祝曰 - Formal expression of the Yellow Dragon holding a cup and standing before the carriage, offering a prayer.
  • 上清神女，玉京仙郎 - Poetic expressions referring to the goddess of the Upper Clarity and the young man of the Jade Capital.
  • 乐此今夕，和鸣凤凰 - Poetic expression indicating joy in the present night, with the phoenixes harmonizing their calls.
  • 凤凰和鸣，将翱将翔 - Poetic expression suggesting that the phoenixes will fly and soar together.
  • 与天齐休，庆流无央 - Poetic expression indicating that their joy will last forever, with endless blessings.
  • 仙郎即以鲛绡五千匹、海人文锦三千端、琉璃琥珀器一百床、明月骊珠各十斛，赠奏乐仙女 - Detailed list of gifts given by the young man to the music-playing fairy.
  • 乃有四鹤立于车前，载仙郎并相者、侍者，兼有宝花台 - Poetic description of four cranes standing before the carriage, carrying the young man, his attendants, and a treasure flower platform.
  • 俄进法膳，凡数十味 - Poetic expression indicating that a variety of dishes were quickly served.
  • 亦沾及韶 - Poetic expression indicating that even the music was affected.
  • 韶袄，有仙女捧玉箱，托红笺笔砚而至，请催妆诗 - Poetic description of a fairy presenting a jade box and red paper with ink and brush, requesting a poem to hasten the makeup.
  • 于是，刘纲诗曰 - Formal expression indicating that Liu Gang then recited a poem.
  • 玉为质兮花为颜，蝉为鬓兮云为环 - Poetic expression describing the beauty of the person, with jade as the substance, flowers as the face, cicadas as the hair, and clouds as the ring.
  • 何劳傅粉兮施渥丹，早出娉婷兮缥缈间 - Poetic expression indicating that there is no need to apply powder or rouge, as the person is already beautiful and graceful.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 於是 - Temporal conjunction meaning 'at that time' or 'then.'
  • 水精帳 - Refers to a crystal-clear or translucent canopy, often associated with divine or celestial settings.
  • 銀燭明 - Describes a silver candle burning brightly, symbolizing purity and illumination.
  • 鳳搖珠佩 - Metaphorical expression where phoenixes shake their pearl ornaments, suggesting elegance and grace.
  • 雲清 - Refers to clouds being clear or pure, often used to describe a serene and ethereal atmosphere.
  • 休匀紅粉飾花態 - Advises against using makeup to enhance one's appearance, emphasizing natural beauty.
  • 早驾双鸾朝玉京 - Urges early departure to ride a pair of phoenixes to the Jade Capital (a metaphor for heaven).

  • 中外之有识者责恕曰 - Literal: Those with insight inside and outside the family reproached Shu saying. Indicates a reproach from people with knowledge.
  • 君家诚贫，乡里岂无贫子弟 - Literal: Your family is indeed poor, is there no poor young man in the village? Rhetorical question challenging the decision.
  • 奈何以女妻园叟 - Literal: Why give your daughter to an old gardener? Expresses surprise and disapproval.
  • 既弃之，何不令远去也 - Literal: Since you have abandoned him, why not let him go far away? Rhetorical question suggesting a solution.
  • 数年，中外之有识者责恕曰 - Literal: Several years later, those with insight inside and outside the family reproached Shu saying. Indicates ongoing criticism.
  • 他日，恕置酒召女及张老 - Literal: One day, Shu set up a feast and invited his daughter and Zhang Lao. Indicates a formal gathering.
  • 酒酣，微露其意 - Literal: When the wine was flowing freely, he subtly revealed his intention. Shows a veiled message.
  • 所以不即去者，恐有念 - Literal: The reason he did not leave immediately is that he might have some attachment. Indicates a possible reason for staying.
  • 今既相厌，去亦何难 - Literal: Now that you are tired of each other, leaving should not be difficult. Suggests that separation is now acceptable.
  • 某王屋下有一小庄 - Literal: Under a certain Wang's house, there is a small farm. Indicates a specific location.
  • 明日且归耳 - Literal: I will return tomorrow. Indicates a temporary departure.
  • 他岁想念，可令大兄往天坛山南相访 - Literal: In other years if you miss me, you can have your older brother visit me in the south of Tian Tan Mountain. Suggests a way to stay in touch.

  • 万象回薄化无方 - Refers to the transformation of all things without fixed form.
  • 天荡荡望沧沧 - Describes the boundless and distant view of the sky and sea.
  • 乘桴轻漾著日傍 - Means to ride a raft gently floating near the sun.
  • 心知和乐悦未央 - Expresses the feeling of peace, joy, and endless delight.
  • 俗谓游乐之处为桑中也 - A common saying that refers to places of leisure as '桑中' (mulberry groves).
  • 期我乎桑中 - From the *Book of Songs* (*Shijing*), meaning 'to meet me in the mulberry grove.'
  • 四维八埏眇难极 - Refers to the eight corners of the universe, which are hard to reach.
  • 驱光逐影穷水域 - Means to chase light and shadows to the ends of the water.
  • 璇宫夜静当轩织 - Describes a quiet palace at night where weaving takes place.
  • 桐峰文梓千寻直 - Refers to tall, straight trees of fine wood.
  • 伐梓作器成琴瑟 - Means to cut down the trees and make them into musical instruments like zithers and harps.
  • 清歌流畅乐难极 - Describes clear singing that flows smoothly and brings endless joy.
  • 沧湄海浦来栖息 - Refers to the shores of the sea where one can rest.
  • 金鸣于山，银涌于地 - Describes gold ringing in the mountains and silver gushing from the ground.
  • 或如龟蛇之类，乍似人鬼之形 - Refers to things resembling turtles, snakes, or human and ghostly forms.
  • 有水屈曲，亦如龙凤之状 - Describes winding water that resembles dragons and phoenixes.
  • 有山盘纤，亦如屈龙之势 - Describes winding mountains that resemble coiled dragons.
  • 龙山龟山凤水之目也 - Refers to the names of mountains and waterways.

  • 金穷氏 - Another name for the Yellow Emperor, possibly emphasizing his association with metal.

  • 因曰凤鸟氏 - Therefore, they were called the Phoenix Clan.
  • 龙丘氏 - A surname, possibly derived from the place name '龙丘.'
  • 西王母《神异传》 - A legendary text about the Queen Mother of the West and her supernatural stories.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 手格猛兽 - To wrestle with fierce beasts, indicating physical prowess.
  • 抚梁易柱 - To lift beams and replace pillars, suggesting great strength and agility.
  • 智足以拒谏 - Intelligence sufficient to reject advice, indicating stubbornness.
  • 言足以饰非 - Speech sufficient to cover up mistakes, indicating skill in justifying wrong actions.
  • 矜人臣以能 - To show off one's abilities to ministers, indicating arrogance.
  • 高天下以声 - To raise one's reputation above the world, indicating a desire for fame.
  • 好酒淫乐 - Enjoying alcohol and excessive pleasure, indicating a love for indulgence.
  • 嬖于有苏之美女妲己 - Favoring the beautiful lady Xi from the state of Yu, indicating a preference for her over others.
  • 惟嬖己言是从 - To follow only what Lady Xi says, indicating blind obedience.
  • 使师涓作新淫之声 - To have Shi Juan compose new licentious music, indicating the creation of morally corrupt music.
  • 北里之舞 - A type of dance associated with the northern part of the city, often considered licentious.
  • 靡靡之乐 - Soft, seductive music, often associated with moral decay.
  • 益收狗马奇物 - To collect more dogs, horses, and strange objects, indicating a love for luxury items.
  • 广沙丘苑台 - To expand the sandy hill garden and terrace, indicating the construction of elaborate structures.
  • 多取野兽飞鸟置其中 - To gather many wild animals and birds and place them there, indicating the collection of exotic animals.
  • 大聚乐戏于沙丘 - To gather for great entertainment and games at the sandy hill, indicating indulgence in leisure activities.
  • 以酒为池，悬肉为林 - To use wine as a pool and hang meat as a forest, indicating extreme luxury and decadence.
  • 使男女裸相逐 - To have men and women run naked, indicating immoral behavior.
  • 为长夜之饮 - To engage in all-night drinking, indicating excessive alcohol consumption.
  • 鄂侯、西伯昌、九侯为三公 - The three highest-ranking officials in the Shang court.
  • 九侯有好女人之纣 - The Marquis of Jiu had a beautiful woman who pleased King Zhou, indicating a gift of a concubine.
  • 九侯女不喜淫 - The daughter of the Marquis of Jiu did not enjoy licentiousness, indicating her moral integrity.
  • 纣怒杀之，而醢九侯 - King Zhou was angry and killed her, and minced the Marquis of Jiu, indicating extreme cruelty.
  • 鄂侯争之，强辩之疾，并脯鄂侯 - Marquis of E argued with him, and his strong defense angered King Zhou, who then minced Marquis of E, indicating further cruelty.
  • 西伯闻之窃叹 - King Wen of Zhou heard about it and sighed secretly, indicating his disapproval.
  • 崇侯虎知之以告纣 - Marquis of Chonghou knew about it and reported it to King Zhou, indicating betrayal.
  • 纣囚西怕里九年 - King Zhou imprisoned King Wen of Zhou in the Li city for nine years, indicating imprisonment.
  • 西怕之臣阂夭之徒，求有莘氏之美女，骊戎之文马，有熊九驷，珍奇怪物，因殷嬖臣费仲献之纣 - The ministers of King Wen of Zhou, including He Tiao, sought beautiful women from the state of Shen, fine horses from Li Rong, nine teams of horses from Youxiong, and rare and strange objects, and through the favored minister Feizong of Yin, they presented them to King Zhou, indicating diplomatic efforts.
  • 纣大悦曰：“此一物足以释西伯，况其多乎。”乃赦西伯，赐之弓矢斧钺，得专征伐 - King Zhou was very pleased and said, 'One of these things is enough to release King Wen of Zhou, let alone so many.' He then released King Wen of Zhou and granted him bows, arrows, axes, and钺, allowing him to conduct independent military campaigns, indicating a change in attitude.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 商王纣 - Refers to King Zhou of Shang, the last ruler of the Shang dynasty, known for his tyranny and the eventual fall of the Shang.
  • 妲己 - A concubine of King Zhou, often portrayed as a seductress who led the king to his downfall.
  • 有苏 - A state that provided King Zhou with the concubine Lady Xi.
  • 师涓 - A musician in the Shang court, known for composing licentious music.
  • 鄂侯、西伯昌、九侯 - The three highest-ranking officials in the Shang court, who were all punished by King Zhou.
  • 西伯昌 - Later known as King Wen of Zhou, the founder of the Zhou dynasty.
  • 崇侯虎 - A minister who betrayed King Wen of Zhou to King Zhou.
  • 有莘氏 - A state that provided King Wen of Zhou with beautiful women.
  • 骊戎 - A state known for its fine horses.
  • 有熊 - A region known for its horses.
  • 费仲 - A favored minister of King Zhou who facilitated the presentation of gifts to King Zhou.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夏后氏衰 - Indicates the decline of the Xia Dynasty.
  • 有二神龙止于夏帝庭而言曰 - Literal description of two divine dragons appearing in the court of the Xia emperor and speaking.
  • 余褒之二君 - Ambiguous phrase, likely refers to the two gods of the state of Bao.
  • 布币而策告之 - Means to place money and make a ritual offering to the dragons.
  • 龙亡而在，椟而藏之 - The dragons disappeared but left behind something that was placed in a box and hidden.
  • 流于庭，不可除 - The object (possibly a dragon or its offspring) appeared in the courtyard and could not be removed.
  • 妇人裸而噪之 - Women were stripped naked and shouted at the object, causing it to transform.
  • 惧而弃之 - The mother of the child was afraid and abandoned the child.
  • 弧箕服，实亡周国 - A children's rhyme predicting the downfall of the Zhou dynasty.
  • 执而戮之 - Captured and killed the couple selling the object.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 夏后氏 - Refers to the Xia Dynasty, the first Chinese dynasty.
  • 夏帝 - The ruler of the Xia Dynasty.
  • 褒姒 - A historical figure, the favorite concubine of King You of Zhou, known for her role in the fall of the Western Zhou Dynasty.
  • 厉王 - King Li of Zhou, a ruler of the Western Zhou Dynasty.
  • 宣王 - King Xuan of Zhou, who succeeded King Li.
  • 弧箕服 - A metaphorical expression that can be interpreted as a reference to a bow and arrow, symbolizing military power.
  • 童女谣 - A children's rhyme that often carried prophetic or ominous meanings in ancient China.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '余褒之二君' is ambiguous and could mean 'the two gods of Bao' or 'the two lords of Bao.'
  • The sentence '流于庭，不可除' is elliptical and could be interpreted as the object appearing in the courtyard and remaining there despite attempts to remove it.
  • The sentence '惧而弃之' is elliptical and could be interpreted as the mother being afraid of the child and abandoning it.
  • The sentence '弧箕服，实亡周国' is elliptical and could be interpreted as the rhyme predicting the downfall of the Zhou dynasty due to the object (or the child).

Proper Nouns

  • 夏后氏 - The Xia Dynasty.
  • 夏帝 - The ruler of the Xia Dynasty.
  • 厉王 - King Li of Zhou.
  • 宣王 - King Xuan of Zhou.
  • 褒姒 - A historical figure, the favorite concubine of King You of Zhou.
  • 弧箕服 - A metaphorical expression referring to a bow and arrow.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '王以与连尹襄老' could be interpreted as 'The king gave it to Lian Yin Xiang Lao' or 'The king gave it to the office of Lian Yin Xiang Lao,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '其子黑要焉' could be interpreted as 'His son, He Yao, did so' or 'His son, He Yao, was involved,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '巫臣使道焉' could be interpreted as 'Wu Chen sent a message to him' or 'Wu Chen sent someone to guide him,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '吾聘汝' could be interpreted as 'I will marry you' or 'I will seek your hand in marriage,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '又使自郑召之' could be interpreted as 'He also sent someone from Zheng to call him' or 'He also sent someone from Zheng to summon him,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '其必因郑而归子' could be interpreted as 'He will surely return you through Zheng' or 'He will surely return you to Zheng,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '与襄老之尸以求之' could be interpreted as 'With the corpse of Xiang Lao to seek him' or 'With the corpse of Xiang Lao to negotiate with him,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '郑人惧于之役' could be interpreted as 'The people of Zheng feared the campaign' or 'The people of Zheng feared the outcome of the campaign,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '欲求媚于晋' could be interpreted as 'They wanted to curry favor with Jin' or 'They wanted to gain favor with Jin,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '王遣夏姬归' could be interpreted as 'The king sent Xia Ji back' or 'The king sent Xia Ji to return,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '将行，谓送者曰' could be interpreted as 'Before leaving, he said to those who were seeing him off' or 'Before leaving, he instructed those who were seeing him off,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '吾不返矣' could be interpreted as 'I will not return' or 'I will not come back,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '巫臣聘诸郑' could be interpreted as 'Wu Chen sought the hand of a woman from Zheng' or 'Wu Chen proposed to a woman from Zheng,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '郑伯许之' could be interpreted as 'The ruler of Zheng agreed' or 'The ruler of Zheng consented,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '将为阳桥之役' could be interpreted as 'He was about to undertake the Yang Bridge campaign' or 'He was about to launch the Yang Bridge campaign,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '使屈巫聘于齐' could be interpreted as 'He sent Qu Wu to propose to Qi' or 'He sent Qu Wu to negotiate with Qi,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '且告师期' could be interpreted as 'And to inform them of the timing of the campaign' or 'And to inform them of the schedule of the campaign,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '巫臣尽室以行' could be interpreted as 'Wu Chen took his entire household with him' or 'Wu Chen brought his entire family with him,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '申叔跪从其父' could be interpreted as 'Shen Shu Gui followed his father' or 'Shen Shu Gui accompanied his father,' depending on the context.
  • The phrase '将适郢' could be interpreted as 'He was about to go to Ying' or 'He was about to travel to Ying,' depending on the context.

  • 昭王知其神异，处于崇霞之台，设枕席以寝宴，遣侍人以卫之 - Indicates that King Zhao recognizes the supernatural nature of the event and arranges for a special pavilion for the banquet.

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不韦因使其姊说夫人曰 - Lü Buwei then had his elder sister persuade Lady Huayang. '因' indicates a sequence of events; '说' means to persuade or convince; '曰' introduces direct speech.
  • 以色事人者，色衰而爱弛 - Those who serve others with beauty, when their beauty fades, their love diminishes. '以色事人' refers to reliance on physical appearance; '色衰而爱弛' is a common idiom in Classical Chinese literature.
  • 今夫人事太子，甚爱而无子 - Now Lady Huayang is deeply loved by the Crown Prince but has no children. '甚爱' emphasizes the extent of the Crown Prince's affection; '无子' is a critical issue in the context of succession.
  • 不以此时早自结于诸子中贤孝者，举立以为适而子之 - If she does not seize this opportunity to secure herself with the most virtuous and filial of the Crown Prince’s sons, and make one of them her own heir, then when the Crown Prince is alive, she will be respected, and after his death, the one she has raised will become king, ensuring she never loses her influence. '早自结' emphasizes the urgency of the action; '举立以为适而子之' is a complex construction meaning to adopt and raise a son as her own.
  • 夫在则重尊，夫百岁之后，所子者为王，终不失势 - While the Crown Prince is alive, she will be honored, and after his death, the one she has raised will become king, ensuring she never loses her power. '重尊' indicates high status; '终不失势' emphasizes the lasting influence.
  • 不以繁华时树本，即色衰爱弛后，虽欲开一语，尚可得乎 - If she does not establish her roots while in her prime, once her beauty fades and her love diminishes, even if she tries to speak, can she still succeed? '树本' metaphorically means to secure her position; '虽欲开一语，尚可得乎' is a rhetorical question emphasizing the difficulty of regaining influence.
  • 今子楚贤而自知，中男也，次不得为适，其母又不得幸 - Now Zi Chu is virtuous and self-aware, he is the middle son, and therefore not the first in line, and his mother is not favored. '自知' indicates Zi Chu’s understanding of his position; '中男' refers to his rank among the Crown Prince’s sons; '不得为适' emphasizes his lack of primogeniture status; '不得幸' refers to his mother’s lack of influence with the Crown Prince.
  • 自附夫人，夫人诚以此时拔以为适，夫人则竟世有宠于秦矣 - If he were to attach himself to Lady Huayang, and she truly were to elevate him as her own heir, then she would remain favored in Qin for all time. '自附' indicates Zi Chu’s willingness to align with Lady Huayang; '拔以为适' is a key action in the plan; '竟世有宠' emphasizes the lasting influence.
  • 华阳夫人以为然 - Lady Huayang thought this was a good idea. '以为然' indicates her agreement with the plan.
  • 乘太子闲，从容言 - Seizing a moment when the Crown Prince was idle, she spoke gently. '乘太子闲' indicates a strategic timing; '从容言' emphasizes the tactful manner of the conversation.
  • 子楚，质于赵者绝贤，来往者皆称誉之 - Zi Chu, who was held hostage in Zhao, is exceptionally virtuous, and all who have met him praise him. '质于赵' refers to Zi Chu’s status as a hostage in the state of Zhao; '绝贤' emphasizes his moral qualities; '来往者皆称誉之' indicates his reputation.
  • 因涕泣曰 - She then wept and said. '涕泣' indicates emotional expression.
  • 妾幸得充后宫，不幸无子，愿得子楚立以为适嗣，以托妾身 - I am fortunate to be in the imperial harem, but unfortunate to have no children. I wish to have Zi Chu adopted as my heir, so that I can entrust my future to him. '充后宫' refers to her status as a concubine; '托妾身' indicates her desire for security and influence.
  • 安国君许之 - Prince An agreed. '许之' indicates his approval of the plan.
  • 乃与夫人刻玉符，约以为适嗣 - He then gave Lady Huayang a jade talisman, making an agreement that Zi Chu would be her heir. '刻玉符' is a symbolic act to seal the agreement; '约以为适嗣' formalizes the plan.
  • 安国君及夫人，因厚馈遗子楚，而请吕不韦傅之 - Prince An and Lady Huayang then lavished gifts on Zi Chu and requested that Lü Buwei be appointed as his tutor. '厚馈遗' indicates their generosity; '傅之' emphasizes the educational support.
  • 子楚以此名誉益盛于诸侯 - As a result, Zi Chu’s reputation grew even more among the feudal lords. '名誉益盛' emphasizes the positive impact of the plan.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 不韦 - Lü Buwei, a prominent statesman and merchant who played a key role in the rise of the Qin state.
  • 华阳夫人 - Lady Huayang, a concubine of the Crown Prince of Qin who later became the Queen Dowager.
  • 子楚 - Zi Chu, the future King Zhuangxiang of Qin, who was later adopted by Lady Huayang.
  • 安国君 - Prince An, the Crown Prince of Qin and father of Zi Chu.
  • 吕不韦 - Lü Buwei, who later became the chancellor of Qin and played a significant role in the unification of China.
  • 质于赵 - Being held hostage in Zhao, a common practice in ancient China to ensure loyalty or peace.
  • 玉符 - A jade talisman, often used in ancient China as a symbol of authority or agreement.
  • 适嗣 - 'heir,' specifically referring to the firstborn son in the context of succession.

  • 知有身 - To know she was pregnant.
  • 因起为寿请之 - To rise and propose a toast, requesting her.
  • 念业已破家为子楚 - To think that he had already spent his fortune on Zi Chu.
  • 欲以钓奇 - To want to attract something extraordinary.
  • 乃遂献其姬 - And thus presented her to him.
  • 姬自匿有娠 - The woman concealed her pregnancy.
  • 至大期时生子政 - When the full term arrived, she gave birth to a son named Zheng.
  • 秦昭王五十年 - In the fiftieth year of King Zhaoxiang of Qin.
  • 使玉围邯郸急 - To send troops to besiege Handan fiercely.
  • 行金六百斤予守者吏 - To give 600 catties of gold to the guards.
  • 得脱亡赴秦军 - To escape and join the Qin army.
  • 遂以得归 - And thus returned.
  • 赵欲杀子楚妻子 - Zhao wanted to kill Zi Chu’s wife and child.
  • 子楚夫人赵豪家女也 - Zi Chu’s wife was a daughter of a wealthy family in Zhao.
  • 得匿，以故母子竟得活 - She managed to hide, and thus mother and child were saved.
  • 秦昭王五十六年薨 - In the fifty-sixth year of King Zhaoxiang of Qin, he died.
  • 太子安国君立为王 - The crown prince An Guo Jun ascended the throne as king.
  • 华阳夫人为王后 - The wife of Huayang became the queen.
  • 子楚为太子 - Zi Chu became the crown prince.
  • 赵亦奉子楚夫人及子政归秦 - Zhao also sent Zi Chu’s wife and son Zheng back to Qin.
  • 秦王立，一年薨，谥为孝文王 - King Qin ascended the throne, and after one year, he died, posthumously titled Emperor Xiao Wen.
  • 太子子楚代立，是为庄襄王 - The crown prince Zi Chu succeeded him, becoming Emperor Zhuang Xiang.
  • 所养母华阳后为华阳太后 - The woman who raised him, the wife of Huayang, became Empress Dowager Huayang.
  • 真母夏姬尊以为夏太后 - His biological mother, Lady Xia Ji, was honored as Empress Dowager Xia.
  • 庄襄王元年，以不韦为丞相，封为文信侯，食河南洛阳十万户 - In the first year of Emperor Zhuang Xiang, Lü Buwei was appointed chancellor, enfeoffed as Duke Wenxin, and granted ten thousand households in Luoyang, Henan.
  • 庄襄王即位三年薨 - After three years, Emperor Zhuang Xiang died.
  • 太子政立为王 - The crown prince Zheng ascended the throne as king.
  • 尊不韦为相国，号称仲父 - Lü Buwei was honored as chancellor and called the second father.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吕不韦 - Lü Buwei, a prominent statesman and merchant during the Warring States period.
  • 子楚 - Zi Chu, later known as Qin Shi Huang’s father.
  • 邯郸 - Handan, the capital of the State of Zhao.
  • 秦昭王 - King Zhaoxiang of Qin, a ruler of the State of Qin.
  • 华阳夫人 - The wife of Huayang, who became the queen of Qin.
  • 庄襄王 - Emperor Zhuang Xiang, the father of Qin Shi Huang.
  • 夏姬 - Lady Xia Ji, the biological mother of Qin Shi Huang.

Proper Nouns

  • 吕不韦 - Lü Buwei
  • 子楚 - Zi Chu
  • 邯郸 - Handan
  • 秦昭王 - King Zhaoxiang of Qin

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 窃私通 - Euphemism for an illicit affair; describes the private and improper relationship between the young Qin King and his mother, the Dowager太后, with Lü Buwei.
  • 羞不如 - Indicates Lü Buwei's feeling of shame or embarrassment at not being as powerful or influential as the other prominent statesmen of the time.
  • 淫不止 - Suggests the Dowager太后's continued indulgence in sexual affairs despite the growing strength of the First Emperor.
  • 大阴人 - A euphemism for a eunuch, used to describe a personal attendant appointed by Lü Buwei.
  • 给事中 - A high-ranking official position in the imperial court, indicating the eunuch's elevated status.
  • 拔其须眉为宦者 - Describes the process of castrating the official to make him a eunuch, allowing him to serve the Dowager太后.
  • 诈卜当避时 - Reflects the use of false divination to justify moving to a different palace to avoid detection and maintain privacy.
  • 徙宫居雍 - Refers to the Dowager太后's move to a different palace, likely to avoid detection and maintain her privacy.
  • 赏赐甚厚 - Indicates the generous rewards given to the eunuch, reflecting his importance and influence.
  • 事皆决于 - Suggests that the eunuch had significant decision-making power, indicating his high status in the court.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦王年少 - Refers to the young age of the Qin King, who was likely a minor when he ascended the throne.
  • 信陵君 - Wei Wuji, one of the Four Princes of the Warring States period, known for his patronage of scholars and influence.
  • 春申君 - Huang Xie, one of the Four Princes of the Warring States period, known for his patronage of scholars and influence.
  • 平原君 - Zhao Sheng, one of the Four Princes of the Warring States period, known for his patronage of scholars and influence.
  • 孟尝君 - Tian Wen, one of the Four Princes of the Warring States period, known for his patronage of scholars and influence.
  • 始皇帝 - The First Emperor of Qin, also known as Qin Shi Huang, a powerful and ambitious ruler.
  • 雍 - A city in the Qin state, where the Dowager太后 moved to avoid detection and maintain her privacy.

Proper Nouns

  • 秦王 - The young king of the Qin state.
  • 太后 - The Dowager太后, the mother of the Qin King.
  • 不韦 - Lü Buwei, a powerful statesman and advisor to the Qin King.
  • 信陵君 - Wei Wuji, one of the Four Princes of the Warring States period.
  • 春申君 - Huang Xie, one of the Four Princes of the Warring States period.
  • 平原君 - Zhao Sheng, one of the Four Princes of the Warring States period.
  • 孟尝君 - Tian Wen, one of the Four Princes of the Warring States period.
  • 始皇帝 - The First Emperor of Qin, also known as Qin Shi Huang.
  • 雍 - A city in the Qin state, where the Dowager太后 moved to avoid detection.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夷三族 - Severe punishment involving execution of the convicted individual and their extended family members, typically three generations.
  • 遂遷太后於雍 - "遂" indicates a sequence of actions, meaning 'then' or 'thereupon.' '遷太后' means 'moved the太后' to a place, in this case, '雍.'
  • 諸舍人，皆沒其家而遷之蜀 - "沒其家" means 'confiscated their families' or 'took their families as captives.' '遷之蜀' means 'moved them to Shu.'
  • 為其奉先王功大 - "為其" means 'because of' or 'due to.' '奉先王' means 'served the late king.'
  • 致法 - "致法" means 'to apply the law' or 'to bring legal punishment.'
  • 相工姚翁善相人 - "相工" refers to a fortune teller or physiognomist. '善相人' means 'skilled at reading people's faces or physiognomy.'
  • 天下貴人也，當生天子 - "天下貴人" means 'a person of great importance in the world.' '當生天子' means 'will give birth to an emperor.'
  • 納太子宮 - "納太子宮" means 'entered the palace as the太子's concubine.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦王 - Refers to Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of China.
  • 太后 - Refers to Empress Dowager Zhao, the mother of Qin Shi Huang.
  • 吕不韦 - A prominent statesman and chancellor during the Qin dynasty, known for his role in the rise of Qin Shi Huang.
  • 汉景帝 - Refers to Emperor Jing of Han, the ruler of the Han dynasty.
  • 王皇后 - Refers to Empress Wang, the wife of Emperor Jing of Han.
  • 金王孙 - A man who was married to Wang Mei'er before she became the empress.
  • 高祖 - Refers to Emperor Gaozu of Han, the founder of the Han dynasty.
  • 武帝 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Han, the son of Emperor Jing of Han and Empress Wang.

Proper Nouns

  • 秦王 - Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of China.
  • 太后 - Empress Dowager Zhao, the mother of Qin Shi Huang.
  • 吕不韦 - A prominent statesman and chancellor during the Qin dynasty.
  • 汉景帝 - Emperor Jing of Han, the ruler of the Han dynasty.
  • 王皇后 - Empress Wang, the wife of Emperor Jing of Han.
  • 金王孙 - A man who was married to Wang Mei'er before she became the empress.
  • 高祖 - Emperor Gaozu of Han, the founder of the Han dynasty.
  • 武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han, the son of Emperor Jing of Han and Empress Wang.

[208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少而聪明，有智术 - Young and intelligent, possessed of wisdom and strategy.
  • 善征其意而应之 - To accurately gauge or understand their intentions and respond to them.
  • 大小皆得其欢心 - Both young and old were pleased with him.
  • 恭敬应对，有若成人 - Responded with respect and propriety, as if he were an adult.
  • 长公主前纳美人，得幸于上，子何不私谒长公主结之乎 - Princess Piao previously took in a concubine who gained favor with the emperor; why don't you privately visit Princess Piao and form a connection with her?
  • 长公主亦怒，工夫人因厚事之 - Princess Piao was also angry; Lady Gong treated her well.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 帝 - Refers to Emperor Liu Bang, the founder of the Han Dynasty.
  • 薄皇后 - Empress Bo, the first empress of Emperor Liu Bang.
  • 栗姬 - Lady Li, a concubine of Emperor Liu Bang who bore him a son.
  • 长公主嫖 - Princess Piao, the eldest daughter of Emperor Liu Bang.
  • 胶东王 - Prince of Jiaodong, a title given to Liu Fei, the son of Lady Li.
  • 漪兰殿 - A palace within the imperial complex where the emperor's children were born.
  • 太子 - The crown prince, the heir apparent to the throne.
  • 长御 - Attendants or maidservants in the imperial court.

Proper Nouns

  • 帝 - Emperor Liu Bang
  • 薄皇后 - Empress Bo
  • 栗姬 - Lady Li
  • 长公主嫖 - Princess Piao
  • 胶东王 - Prince of Jiaodong (Liu Fei)
  • 漪兰殿 - A palace within the imperial complex
  • 太子 - Crown prince
  • 长御 - Attendants or maidservants
